headline: API and multi-agent systems lead the week as token demand breaks out and LLM career anxiety surfaces.
body: API and multi-agent systems hold the leading positions this window, with model release close behind, while AI engineering, search engine, IPO, and edge AI all weaken. On the technique side token demand is breaking out and cost analysis, image generation API, arithmetic, LLM, flow matching, automated testing, and quality control all enter as new. The discussion thread runs toward developer economics and displacement: one engineer says LLMs are eroding their software career [8], a bootstrapped founder reports that the ROI of AI coding looks different under resource constraints [5], and OpenAI signals a shift away from chat toward a super app, with a senior employee declaring chat dead [3]. Smaller items cover a Notion service disruption affecting Anthropic access [2], a request for an official Claude Desktop on Linux [7], and a question about how configurable thinking effort is implemented [9].

cited_items.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
Ask HN: How are thinking efforts implemented?
